text_original: Motse said ; Before the art of building houses and palaces was known primitive people lingered by the mounds and lived in caves.
text_normalized: Motse said ; Before the art of building houses and palaces was known primitive people lingered by the mounds and lived in caves.

text_original: It was damp and injurious to health.
text_normalized: It was damp and injurious to health.

text_original: There-upon the sage-kings  built houses and palaces.
text_normalized: There-upon the sage-kings  built houses and palaces.

text_original: The guiding principles for these buildings were these : The house shall be built high enough to avoid the damp and moisture ; the walls thick enough to keep out the wind and cold ; the roof strong enough to stand snow, frost, rain, and dew ; and the walls in the palace high enough to observe the propriety of the sexes.
text_normalized: The guiding principles for these buildings were these : The house shall be built high enough to avoid the damp and moisture ; the walls thick enough to keep out the wind and cold ; the roof strong enough to stand snow, frost, rain, and dew ; and the walls in the palace high enough to observe the propriety of the sexes.

text_original: These are suiSScient, and any expenditure of money and energy that does not bring additional utility shall not be permitted.
text_normalized: These are suiSScient, and any expenditure of money and energy that does not bring additional utility shall not be permitted.

text_original: When the city walls are repaired with regular labour, the people may feel tired but there is no exhaustion.
text_normalized: When the city walls are repaired with regular labour, the people may feel tired but there is no exhaustion.

text_original: When taxes are collected according to custom, the people may be deprived of some money but there is no bitterness.
text_normalized: When taxes are collected according to custom, the people may be deprived of some money but there is no bitterness.

text_original: The real woe of the people does not lie here, it lies in heavy taxes.
text_normalized: The real woe of the people does not lie here, it lies in heavy taxes.

text_original: The sage-kings built houses and palaces in order to better the living conditions and not for pleasures of sight.
text_normalized: The sage-kings built houses and palaces in order to better the living conditions and not for pleasures of sight.

text_original: They made clothes and hats, belts and shoes in order to protect the body and not for novelty.
text_normalized: They made clothes and hats, belts and shoes in order to protect the body and not for novelty.

text_original: They were thrifty themselves and taught the people to be the same.
text_normalized: They were thrifty themselves and taught the people to be the same.

text_original: And, so, the people in the whole world became orderly and wealth was sufficient for use.
text_normalized: And, so, the people in the whole world became orderly and wealth was sufficient for use.

text_original: When tile present rulers' build tbeir residences, tbey are quite'' different : from this.
text_normalized: When tile present rulers' build tbeir residences, tbey are quite'' different : from this.

text_original: They would heavily tax the people, robbing them’ of their means of livelihood, in ordei' to have' their palaces 'covered with porches and pavilions in vario'iis designs, and adorned with paintings and sculpture.
text_normalized: They would heavily tax the people, robbing them’ of their means of livelihood, in ordei' to have' their palaces 'covered with porches and pavilions in vario'iis designs, and adorned with paintings and sculpture.

text_original: When the ruler builds his palaces in this fashion, his assistants , naturally imitate ■ him.
text_normalized: When the ruler builds his palaces in this fashion, his assistants , naturally imitate ■ him.

text_original: And, so, there is not sufficient wealth to prepare against famine and dearth or to relieve the orphans and widows.
text_normalized: And, so, there is not sufficient wealth to prepare against famine and dearth or to relieve the orphans and widows.

text_original: And the people become more unruly as the state becomes poorer.
text_normalized: And the people become more unruly as the state becomes poorer.

text_original: If the rulers sincerely desire to have the empire orderly and hate to see it in disorder, they must not indulge in building houses and palaces.
text_normalized: If the rulers sincerely desire to have the empire orderly and hate to see it in disorder, they must not indulge in building houses and palaces.

text_original: Before clothing was known the primitive people wore coats of furs and belts of straw.
text_normalized: Before clothing was known the primitive people wore coats of furs and belts of straw.

text_original: They were neither light and warm in winter nor light and cool in summer.
text_normalized: They were neither light and warm in winter nor light and cool in summer.

text_original: The sage-Mng thought this did not satisfy the needs of man.
text_normalized: The sage-Mng thought this did not satisfy the needs of man.

text_original: So, he taught the women to produce silk and flax and to weave cloth and linen, therewith to make clothing for the people.
text_normalized: So, he taught the women to produce silk and flax and to weave cloth and linen, therewith to make clothing for the people.

text_original: The guiding principles for clothing were these : In winter the underwear shall be made of spun-silk so as to be light and warm.
text_normalized: The guiding principles for clothing were these : In winter the underwear shall be made of spun-silk so as to be light and warm.

text_original: In summer it shall be made of coarse flax so as to be light and cool.
text_normalized: In summer it shall be made of coarse flax so as to be light and cool.

text_original: And this is sufficient.
text_normalized: And this is sufficient.

text_original: Therefore the sages made their clothes just to fit their stature and size, and not for the purpose of pleasing the senses or to dazzle the common people.
text_normalized: Therefore the sages made their clothes just to fit their stature and size, and not for the purpose of pleasing the senses or to dazzle the common people.

text_original: In that age, durable carts and gentle horses were not valued, neither were sculpture and adornments prized .
text_normalized: In that age, durable carts and gentle horses were not valued, neither were sculpture and adornments prized .

text_original: What is the reason for this ?, The reason lies in the kind of leadersidp.
text_normalized: What is the reason for this ?, The reason lies in the kind of leadersidp.

text_original: Tie people kad sufficient means of livelihood in their home to meet either drought or floods dearth or famine.
text_normalized: Tie people kad sufficient means of livelihood in their home to meet either drought or floods dearth or famine.

text_original: Why ■ Because they imderstood the needs of self-support and paid little attention to external appearance.
text_normalized: Why ■ Because they imderstood the needs of self-support and paid little attention to external appearance.

text_original: So, the people were frugal and orderly and the ruler was thrifty and easily supported.
text_normalized: So, the people were frugal and orderly and the ruler was thrifty and easily supported.

text_original: The store house and treasury were full, prepared against misfortunes.
text_normalized: The store house and treasury were full, prepared against misfortunes.

text_original: Armour and weapons were not left in disuse and the soldiers and the people were not tired, ready to punish the unsubmissive.
text_normalized: Armour and weapons were not left in disuse and the soldiers and the people were not tired, ready to punish the unsubmissive.

text_original: Thus the ruler could become a tyrant over the empire.
text_normalized: Thus the ruler could become a tyrant over the empire.

text_original: The present rulers are quite different from this when they make their clothes.
text_normalized: The present rulers are quite different from this when they make their clothes.

text_original: Having what is warm and light in winter and what is light and cool in summer, they would yet heavily tax the people, robbing them of their means of liveli-hood, in order to have elaborately embroidered and gorgeous garments.
text_normalized: Having what is warm and light in winter and what is light and cool in summer, they would yet heavily tax the people, robbing them of their means of liveli-hood, in order to have elaborately embroidered and gorgeous garments.

text_original: Hooks are made of gold and ornaments on the girdle consist of pearls and jades.
text_normalized: Hooks are made of gold and ornaments on the girdle consist of pearls and jades.

text_original: Women are employed to make the embroidery and men to do the carving.
text_normalized: Women are employed to make the embroidery and men to do the carving.

text_original: All these are for the adornment of the body.
text_normalized: All these are for the adornment of the body.

text_original: They really add little to its warmth.
text_normalized: They really add little to its warmth.

text_original: Wealth is squandered and energy wasted all for naught.
text_normalized: Wealth is squandered and energy wasted all for naught.

text_original: So, then, when clothing is made not for the body but for brilliant appearance, the people will be wicked and unruly and the ruler extravagant and deaf to good counsel.
text_normalized: So, then, when clothing is made not for the body but for brilliant appearance, the people will be wicked and unruly and the ruler extravagant and deaf to good counsel.

text_original: It will be impossible to keep the country out of disorder.
text_normalized: It will be impossible to keep the country out of disorder.

text_original: If the rulers sincerely desire the empire to have order and hate to see it in disorder, they must not indulge in making clothing excessively.
text_normalized: If the rulers sincerely desire the empire to have order and hate to see it in disorder, they must not indulge in making clothing excessively.

text_original: Before the art of cooking was known, primitive people ate only vegetables and lived in separation.
text_normalized: Before the art of cooking was known, primitive people ate only vegetables and lived in separation.

text_original: Thereupon the sage  taught the men to attend to farming and to plant trees to supply the people with food.
text_normalized: Thereupon the sage  taught the men to attend to farming and to plant trees to supply the people with food.

text_original: And the sole purpose of securing food is to increase energy, satisfy hunger, strengthen the body and appease the stomach.
text_normalized: And the sole purpose of securing food is to increase energy, satisfy hunger, strengthen the body and appease the stomach.

text_original: He was frugal in spending wealth and simple in habits of living, and, so, the people became rich and the country orderly.
text_normalized: He was frugal in spending wealth and simple in habits of living, and, so, the people became rich and the country orderly.

text_original: With the present rulers all is different.
text_normalized: With the present rulers all is different.

text_original: They would heavily tax the people in order to enjoy elaborately the different meats and fish and turtle cooked in various ways, (The lord of) a large state is served with a hundred courses and (that of) a small state, with tens of courses, which will cover a table space of ten square feet.
text_normalized: They would heavily tax the people in order to enjoy elaborately the different meats and fish and turtle cooked in various ways, (The lord of) a large state is served with a hundred courses and (that of) a small state, with tens of courses, which will cover a table space of ten square feet.

text_original: The eyes cannot see all the dishes, the hands cannot handle them all, and the mouth cannot taste them all.
text_normalized: The eyes cannot see all the dishes, the hands cannot handle them all, and the mouth cannot taste them all.

text_original: In winter they will freeze, and in summer they sour.
text_normalized: In winter they will freeze, and in summer they sour.

text_original: As the ruler serves himself thus, naturally his assistants imitate him.
text_normalized: As the ruler serves himself thus, naturally his assistants imitate him.

text_original: And so the rich and high in rank are wasteful and extravagant, while the solitary and miserable are hungry and cold.
text_normalized: And so the rich and high in rank are wasteful and extravagant, while the solitary and miserable are hungry and cold.

text_original: It is impossible to keep such a state out of disorder.
text_normalized: It is impossible to keep such a state out of disorder.

text_original: If the rulers sincerely desire the empire to have order and hate to see it in disorder, they must not indulge in excessive eating and drinking.
text_normalized: If the rulers sincerely desire the empire to have order and hate to see it in disorder, they must not indulge in excessive eating and drinking.

text_original: Before the primitive people knew how to make boats and carts they could neither carry a heavy load nor travel a great distance.
text_normalized: Before the primitive people knew how to make boats and carts they could neither carry a heavy load nor travel a great distance.

text_original: Thereupon the sage-king  made boats and carts to facilitate the people.
text_normalized: Thereupon the sage-king  made boats and carts to facilitate the people.

text_original: The boats and carts were made durable and convenient so that they would carry much and travel far.
text_normalized: The boats and carts were made durable and convenient so that they would carry much and travel far.

text_original: Such an undertaking takes little wealth but produces many benefits.
text_normalized: Such an undertaking takes little wealth but produces many benefits.

text_original: Naturally the people found it agreeable and convenient.
text_normalized: Naturally the people found it agreeable and convenient.

text_original: The people were not tired out and yet the ruler’s needs were all supplied, SOj people were attracted to him.
text_normalized: The people were not tired out and yet the ruler’s needs were all supplied, SOj people were attracted to him.

text_original: When the present rulers make boats and carts, it is quite difierent.
text_normalized: When the present rulers make boats and carts, it is quite difierent.

text_original: Having made them durable and convenient, they would yet heavily tax the people to decorate them.
text_normalized: Having made them durable and convenient, they would yet heavily tax the people to decorate them.

text_original: The carts are decorated with embroidery and boats with carving.
text_normalized: The carts are decorated with embroidery and boats with carving.

text_original: Women have to stop weaving to do the embroidery so the people are left cold.
text_normalized: Women have to stop weaving to do the embroidery so the people are left cold.

text_original: While men have to abandon agriculture to do the carving and so the people become hungry.
text_normalized: While men have to abandon agriculture to do the carving and so the people become hungry.

text_original: When the ruler builds such boats and carts for himself, naturally his assistants imitate him.
text_normalized: When the ruler builds such boats and carts for himself, naturally his assistants imitate him.

text_original: Therefore the people become victims of both hunger and cold, and they commit wickedness.
text_normalized: Therefore the people become victims of both hunger and cold, and they commit wickedness.

text_original: Much wickedness is followed by heavy punish-ment, and heavy punishment places the country in disorder.
text_normalized: Much wickedness is followed by heavy punish-ment, and heavy punishment places the country in disorder.

text_original: If the rulers sincerely desire the empire to have order and hate to see it in disorder, they must not indulge themselves in constructing boats and carts excessively.
text_normalized: If the rulers sincerely desire the empire to have order and hate to see it in disorder, they must not indulge themselves in constructing boats and carts excessively.

text_original: Every creature living between Heaven and earth and within the four seas  partakes of the nature of Heaven and earth and the harmony of the Yin and the Yang.
text_normalized: Every creature living between Heaven and earth and within the four seas  partakes of the nature of Heaven and earth and the harmony of the Yin and the Yang.

text_original: Even the greatest sages cannot alter this.
text_normalized: Even the greatest sages cannot alter this.

text_original: How do we know ?
text_normalized: How do we know ?

text_original: When they taught about Heaven and earth, they dwelt on the upper and the lower spheres and the four seasons, the principles of Yin and Yang and human nature, the phenomena of men and women, birds and animals, and that of the sexes.
text_normalized: When they taught about Heaven and earth, they dwelt on the upper and the lower spheres and the four seasons, the principles of Yin and Yang and human nature, the phenomena of men and women, birds and animals, and that of the sexes.

text_original: Even the early kings could not escape from the fundamentals of nature.
text_normalized: Even the early kings could not escape from the fundamentals of nature.

text_original: Even the great sages of ancient times must keep a household.
text_normalized: Even the great sages of ancient times must keep a household.

text_original: Only, as their conduct was not unfavourably affected, there was no dissatisfaction among the people, and as women were not kept within the palace (to be spinsters) there were few bachelors in the empire.
text_normalized: Only, as their conduct was not unfavourably affected, there was no dissatisfaction among the people, and as women were not kept within the palace (to be spinsters) there were few bachelors in the empire.

text_original: As women were not kept within the palace and as there were few bachelors abroad, the population in the empire was large.
text_normalized: As women were not kept within the palace and as there were few bachelors abroad, the population in the empire was large.

text_original: The present rulers of large states retain as many women as a thousand in their household and those of small states as many as a hundred.
text_normalized: The present rulers of large states retain as many women as a thousand in their household and those of small states as many as a hundred.

text_original: Therefore men in the empire axe mostly without wife and women without husband.
text_normalized: Therefore men in the empire axe mostly without wife and women without husband.

text_original: The functions of men and women are prevented and the population becomes small.
text_normalized: The functions of men and women are prevented and the population becomes small.

text_original: If the rulers sincerely desire the population to be large and hate to see it small, they must not indulge in retaining too many women.
text_normalized: If the rulers sincerely desire the population to be large and hate to see it small, they must not indulge in retaining too many women.

text_original: Now, in these five things  the sages are temperate and economical while the wicked men are indulgent and excessive.
text_normalized: Now, in these five things  the sages are temperate and economical while the wicked men are indulgent and excessive.

text_original: Temperance and economy bring prosperity while indulgence and excess lead to destruction.
text_normalized: Temperance and economy bring prosperity while indulgence and excess lead to destruction.

text_original: One must not indulge in excess in these five things.
text_normalized: One must not indulge in excess in these five things.

text_original: When husband and wife do not indulge in excess, Heaven and earth will be harmonious ; when wind and rain are not in excess, the five grains will ripen ; and when excessive clothing is not indulged in, the body will be comfortable.
text_normalized: When husband and wife do not indulge in excess, Heaven and earth will be harmonious ; when wind and rain are not in excess, the five grains will ripen ; and when excessive clothing is not indulged in, the body will be comfortable.
